summary.intent: Fix UI inconsistencies in the browser sessions panel — make the 'New Session' button match the size of 'Refresh', and make 'Refresh' match the style of refresh buttons used on other pages.
summary.outcome: Updated page-browser.js to apply consistent button classes (matching MCP and Skills pages pattern: `provider-btn provider-btn-secondary provider-btn-sm`), committed and pushed the fix.

repo: Refresh buttons across pages (MCP, Skills) use the combined class pattern `provider-btn provider-btn-secondary provider-btn-sm` for consistent sizing and secondary styling.
The browser panel lives in crates/web/src/assets/js/page-browser.js and uses a flex layout with `items-center gap-2` for button grouping.

code.finding: Browser sessions header buttons were using `provider-btn text-xs px-2 py-1` and `provider-btn-secondary text-xs px-2 py-1` instead of the shared `provider-btn-sm` sizing class used elsewhere.

workflow: YubiKey SSH signing requires physical tap for git push; the agent cannot proceed autonomously and must wait for user interaction.
Grepping for existing button patterns across the codebase is the fastest way to identify the correct CSS class conventions before editing.

friction: YubiKey tap requirement blocked the push twice, requiring explicit user intervention before the push could complete.
